betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Centrifuge§f:
The Centrifuge (nuclearcraft:centrifuge) is a single-block machine that uses centrifugal force to separate different isotopes in fluids. It is the fluid-phase counterpart to the Separator (which handles solids).

§6Slot Layout (CentrifugeRecipes.java)§f:
The Centrifuge has 1 fluid input and 4 fluid outputs (or 1 item + 1 fluid input with multiple outputs). It's the highest-output NC machine in terms of distinct product streams.

§6Crafting Recipe (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:118)§f:
Pattern "PFP / MCM / PSP" in a 3×3 grid. Letters: P = plateAdvanced, F = ingotFerroboron, M = motor, C = chassis, S = servo. Yields 1 Centrifuge.

§6What It Does (CentrifugeRecipes.java)§f:
The Centrifuge handles fluid-phase isotope separation:
- §bUranium Hexafluoride → Uranium-235 + Uranium-238 + trace wastes§f
- §bDepleted UF6 → recovery of residual U-235 + U-238 + fluoride carrier§f
- §bPlutonium Hexafluoride → Pu-239 + Pu-240 + Pu-241 + Pu-242 + wastes§f
- §bNeptunium Hexafluoride → Np-236 + Np-237 + fluoride carrier§f
- §bAmericium Hexafluoride → Am-241 + Am-242 + Am-243 + wastes§f
- §bCurium Hexafluoride → Cm-243/245/246/247 + fluoride carrier§f
- §bBerkelium Hexafluoride → Bk-247 + Bk-248 + carrier§f
- §bCalifornium Hexafluoride → Cf-249/251/252 + carrier§f

§6Fluid Production Chain§f:
The Centrifuge requires hexafluoride fluid input. The full chain is:
- §bIngot → Fluid§f: Melter (ingot → molten metal)
- §bMolten metal + Fluorine → Hexafluoride§f: Chemical Reactor
- §bHexafluoride → Isotope streams§f: Centrifuge

§6Direct Descendants of Centrifuge (TaskRetrieval)§f:
The graph shows 5 items used in the Centrifuge recipe:
- plateAdvanced (nuclearcraft:part:1)
- ingotFerroboron (nuclearcraft:alloy:6)
- motor (nuclearcraft:part:8)
- servo (nuclearcraft:part:7)
- chassis (nuclearcraft:part:10)

§6Why It Matters§f:
The Centrifuge handles the §efluid-phase enrichment path§f, which is the route preferred for high-throughput production. Compared to the solid Separator (which processes 10 ingots per cycle), the Centrifuge can process thousands of mB of fluid per cycle, yielding isotope streams at industrial scale.

§6Synergy With Salt Reactor§f:
The Salt Reactor uses fluid fuel (fluoride-FLiBe). After fission, the depleted fluid can be sent directly to the Centrifuge for actinide recovery, no solid/liquid phase change required.

§6Prerequisites§f:
Requires q30021 (Alloy Furnace) — the basic NC unlock.

§eTip§f: For maximum throughput, run a chain: Reactor → Centrifuge → fresh fuel + wastes. This creates a closed-loop actinide economy.

§eTip§f: Build a dedicated fluid tank array for each isotope output. The Centrifuge's 4-output design means you need 4 separate fluid destinations per machine.

§cWarning§f: Centrifuge outputs are intensely radioactive. Use Rad-Shielded fluid tanks (NC:rad_shielding tank variants) for storage.
